scenarios.title: Hormuz closure
scenarios.body: Iran closes or contests the strait → ~20 Mb/d oil + 1/5 of LNG at risk → war-risk insurance 10-50x within days (London prices it first) → Asia refiners scramble, SPR releases, oil +$30-60 → freight + goods inflation global. Watch: Lloyd's rate cards, tanker AIS, US 5th Fleet posture. Duration is everything: a week is a spike; a quarter is a recession.

order_effects.text: Hormuz transit halts — ~20 Mb/d crude + 1/5 of global LNG diverted or delayed
VLCC rates 3–5×; Lloyd's war-risk premium 10–50× within hours; tanker AIS tracking spikes
East-Asia refiners (CHN/IND/JPN/KOR, ~80% of flow) bid up Atlantic-basin crude → US Gulf netbacks rise; SPR draw authorized
Tanker-owner equity (Frontline/Euronav-type) re-rates before spot prints — shipping equity leads crude price by days; shipping insurers face loss-ratio crisis

scenarios.title: Taiwan blockade
scenarios.body: Quarantine/blockade (no invasion needed) → insurance withdrawal halts shipping → TSMC wafers + ASE packaging severed → NVIDIA pipeline stops in weeks; global electronics in months → Luzon cable risk adds data severance. No meaningful substitute capacity exists (AZ fabs: single-digit %, no packaging). This is the tail that re-prices everything; even a 5% annual probability dominates expected-loss math.

order_effects.text: Taiwan Strait blockade — insurance withdrawal halts all commercial shipping; TSMC wafers + ASE packaging severed
NVIDIA GPU pipeline stops in weeks; global AI capex deferred 12–18 months; cloud buildout halts
Cloud providers miss capacity commitments → AI startup valuations re-rate as access becomes the constraint; hyperscaler capex guidance misses for 2 consecutive quarters
Chinese foundries (SMIC) re-rate as partial substitutes despite 2-gen yield lag; the 'second source premium' gets priced globally as a structural cost of non-Taiwan supply chains

scenarios.title: China REE/magnet embargo
scenarios.body: Beijing converts the Apr-2025 licensing regime into denial (response to chip escalation) → NdFeB magnet flow stops → EV motors, wind turbines, defense systems (F-35 uses ~400kg REE) queue behind 3-5yr western separation buildout → MP/Lynas re-rate violently; DoD floors extend to other minerals. The most-rehearsed, most-likely-executed economic weapon in this atlas.

order_effects.text: NdFeB magnet flow from China halts — EV motors, wind turbines, F-35 (uses ~400kg REE) queue behind 3–5yr Western buildout
MP Materials / Lynas re-rate violently as only Western separation sources; DoD emergency floors trigger; magnet prices 3–5×
EU offshore wind delivery schedules slide 18–24 months; 2030 renewable targets miss; utilities re-rate the real cost of energy transition

Western REE separation capacity buildout (MP Texas, Lynas Texas, SRB) attracts $10B+ capital — but 3–5yr lead time means demand-supply gap widens before it closes; China's pricing power persists through the gap

scenarios.title: US power crunch deepens
scenarios.body: DC demand (4.4%→6.7-12% by 2028) meets sold-out turbines + 5yr queues + transformer scarcity → capacity auction prices spike (PJM already 10x) → ratepayer politics turn against DCs → behind-the-meter gas (Abilene model) + restarts (Palisades, Crane) become the only fast paths → power, not chips, sets the AI buildout's clock speed 2026-29. This is the base case, not a tail.

order_effects.text: DC power demand hits grid capacity ceiling — PJM capacity auction prices spike 10×; turbine slots sold out to 2029
Hyperscalers shift to behind-the-meter gas; nuclear restarts (Palisades, Crane) get emergency permitting; GEV/Siemens Energy backlog extends to 2031
AI model training queues extend; inference latency SLAs relax under load; OpenAI/Anthropic/Google announce capacity constraints — hyperscaler capex guidance misses

Ratepayer politics turn against DCs in Virginia/Texas; state legislation caps DC power allotments; new DC construction shifts to Alberta/Quebec hydro — geography of AI compute re-centers on cheap power

scenarios.title: Cable sabotage wave
scenarios.body: Gray-zone actors extend the Baltic/Matsu/Red-Sea pattern → simultaneous cuts at Egypt corridor + Luzon → EU-Asia latency/capacity crisis, cloud + trading reroute via US → repair-ship fleet (~60 aging vessels worldwide) becomes the binding constraint, repairs queue for months → cable-route diversity and repair capacity get repriced as strategic assets. Cheapest scenario to trigger; least modeled by markets.

order_effects.text: Egypt corridor + Luzon cuts simultaneously — EU-Asia latency triples; ~40% transoceanic capacity offline for weeks to months
Repair-ship fleet (~60 aging vessels globally) queues for months; traffic reroutes via US cables → US cloud providers see latency SLA breaches in EU/APAC
HFT and algorithmic-trading firms face asymmetric latency across routes; financial markets price in temporary geographic arbitrage premiums; some exchange connections effectively severed

Subsea cable diversity becomes mandated tier-1 infrastructure (analogue to power redundancy); SubCom/ASN/NEC see 5–10× order bookings; cable insurance reprices as strategic asset class — least-modeled second-order in this atlas
